facts.text: The Babylon Town Historical Society, founded in 2006, calls the historic Van Bourgondien House in West Babylon its headquarters.
Its mission is to collect, preserve, research, and interpret the history of the Town of Babylon, fostering deeper appreciation of the area.
Belmont Junction once hosted a freight depot and telegraph station that operated until 1888, marking West Babylon’s early rail era.
A trolley sign near Trolley Line Road once read “West” toward Amityville, indicating the line that ran between Babylon station and Great East Neck Road.
In 1936, actor James Barton opened the first lighted sports park in Suffolk County, hosting baseball games and wrestling matches in West Babylon.
The first West Babylon school was built in the early 1800s on Montauk Highway near Great East Neck Road, serving the community’s early educational needs.
